findings.evidence: `rg` shows `@[simp] lemma associatedBilinForm_apply_right_apply` at lines 53-55, while `lemma associatedBilinForm_apply_left_apply` at lines 62-64 has no annotation.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `associatedBilinForm_apply_left_apply`; if desired, also make `associatedBilinForm_invariant` a simp lemma once the left/right rewrites have a clear normal form.
findings.issue: `Compatible.associatedBilinForm_apply_left_apply` is the left-argument normal form for the metric, but it is not marked `[simp]`, unlike the parallel right-argument lemma at line 53.

findings.evidence: Lines 114-132 mark `innerProductCore` reducible and then immediately expose `@[simp] lemma innerProductCore_inner : @inner ℝ V h.innerProductCore.toInner v w = ω v (J w)`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[reducible]` and rely on the `[simp]` characteristic lemma `innerProductCore_inner`; add any missing characteristic lemmas instead of making the definition unfold by reducibility.
findings.issue: `innerProductCore` is exported with `@[reducible]`, exposing the body even though the API already provides `innerProductCore_inner` to characterize the inner product.

summary: The exposed metric API is mostly the right surface, but it leaves a key characteristic equation out of simp normal form and marks the core reducible despite providing a characteristic lemma.
